task_completion.description: A track named 'Voiceover' is created in the audio editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a track header or label in the audio editor's timeline area explicitly named 'Voiceover'.

task_completion.description: The narration.wav audio file is imported into the 'Voiceover' track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an audio region/waveform inside the 'Voiceover' track. The region might be labeled 'narration' or 'narration.wav'.

task_completion.description: The audio region starts exactly at 0.0 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the timeline ruler at the top of the track view. The left edge of the audio region must align perfectly with the 0:00:00 (zero) mark.

task_completion.description: The audio is time-stretched to exactly 25.0 seconds without altering pitch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the right edge of the audio region on the timeline. It should align exactly with the 25.0-second mark. There may also be visual indicators of the Time Stretch tool being used (e.g., a stretch percentage on the region, or the stretch tool icon selected in the toolbar).

task_completion.description: The final audio is exported to /home/ga/Audio/export/ad_voiceover_25s.wav.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export dialog box showing the file path '/home/ga/Audio/export/ad_voiceover_25s.wav', a success message indicating the export completed, or a file manager/terminal window showing the exported file in the correct directory.

task_completion.description: The audio editor session is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Session > Save' menu option being clicked, a 'Session saved' notification, or the application title bar showing the session name without an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk '*').

integrity.description: The agent used the audio editor's graphical user interface to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour (or similar audio editor) GUI open with tracks and waveforms visible, rather than just terminal windows running ffmpeg or sox commands.

integrity.description: The audio was genuinely time-stretched, not just trimmed or cut.
integrity.visual_evidence: The waveform should appear complete but compressed to fit the 25-second duration. It should not look like the right side of the waveform was simply sliced off at the 25-second mark.

integrity.description: The export process was initiated through the audio editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the application's native export dialog or progress bar, ensuring the agent didn't just use a terminal command to copy a pre-existing file to the export directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original audio file is exactly 30.0 seconds long. The target duration is strictly 25.0 seconds (with a 0.2s tolerance). The audio must be time-stretched, meaning the entire 30-second waveform is compressed into 25.0 seconds. If the waveform looks like it was simply cut/trimmed at the 25-second mark, the time-stretch requirement was failed. The sample rate is 44100 Hz.
